verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix/inner-membrane-facing protein forming the catalytic F1 head. However, multiple independent surface proteomics, flow cytometry, and live-cell antibody-binding studies have documented ATP5F1B on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — particularly on tumor cells, immune cells, and cells under stress or energy deprivation. This 'ecto-ATP synthase' phenomenon is well-established for the F1-beta subunit specifically; it has been detected on endothelial cells, hepatocytes, adipocytes, and various cancer lines. There is no classical TM domain or GPI anchor — the mechanism of PM translocation is not fully resolved, but the surface pool is wash-resistant in documented studies. This constitutes cell-state-induced and/or tumor-cell outer-leaflet display with experimental evidence specific to this protein.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of plasma-membrane anchoring is unresolved (no TM/GPI); extent to which surface pool is truly wash-resistant vs. in equilibrium with secreted/shed pool remains debated in literature.
